Name: Meinhof Bantu Orthography
IPA_Notes: See Meinhof 1899; used in many early Bantu wordlists

Name: International African Institute Orthography
IPA_Notes: Close to IPA; ŋ, ɲ, ɓ, ɗ used

Name: Arabic-based Ajami Orthography
Key_Features: Hausa phonemes adapted to Arabic script

Name: Ge'ez / Ethiopic Fidel
IPA_Notes: Each Ethiopic char = C+V; map via Unicode tables

Name: Standard Hausa Orthography (Boko)
Key_Features: Hooks for implosives: ɓ=ƃ, ɗ=ƌ, k=ƙ; tones unmarked
IPA_Notes: Implosive hooks→IPA implosives; tones must be checked against source

Name: Standard Yoruba Orthography
Key_Features: Subscript dot for open vowels; acute/grave for tones
IPA_Notes: Dotted vowels→open mid; acute=H, grave=L, unmarked=M

Name: Standard Zulu/Nguni Orthography
IPA_Notes: Click letters map to IPA clicks

Name: Standard Lingala Orthography
Key_Features: Tones largely unmarked in most sources

Name: Fula (Fulfulde) Adlam Script
IPA_Notes: Adlam→IPA table in Unicode Adlam block docs

Name: Custom/Unclassified Orthography
Key_Features: Use when source orthography does not match any registered system
